title: The 2026 Antigua harvest: La Niña and what producers are calling 'unusually clean'
body:
Harvest window: December 2025 – March 2026.

Early arrivals are showing low defect counts across the region. Cooler nights through January, slightly delayed flowering in October, and a dry harvest window all combined into what beneficios are quietly calling a once-in-five-year crop. The Antigua Coffee Producers Association's preliminary cupping shows an average 86.4 across submitted lots, with the top decile crossing 89. Buyers from Tokyo, Oslo, and Melbourne have been on the ground since late January.

title: Acatenango Origin Tasting — November 2025
body: An invitation-only cupping event hosted at Beneficio Bella Vista, bringing together fourteen Antigua producers and twenty-three international roaster representatives. Two cupping rounds: classical washed Bourbon, then experimental anaerobic and carbonic lots. Outcome: three previously unrepresented Antigua farms placed lots with European specialty roasters during the on-site auction phase.

title: Anacafé Sustainability Forum — June 2026
body: Three days at Anacafé's Guatemala City headquarters. Focus: integrated leaf-rust management, the 2026 rust strain's reduced copper sensitivity, and the agency's revised recommendation matrix for resistant varietals. Forty Antigua producers attended; the consensus on the closing day was that the Marsellesa-Castillo-Costa Rica 95 trio remains the practical option, with Centroamericano showing promise for early adopters.

title: Cauca Best of Origin 2026
body: Annual cupping competition held in Popayán. 312 lots submitted; 23 cleared 87, with the winning lot — a 1,820 m anaerobic Castillo — cupping 89.7. Auction prices averaged $14.20/lb, with the top lot clearing $42.10/lb. Two Japanese buyers and a Norwegian roaster participated on the cupping panel. The Cauca Federation's reform discussions overlapped with the event's closing session.

title: Inzá Anaerobic Symposium — March 2026
body: A two-day technical symposium hosted by a coalition of Inzá-area micro-mill operators. Topics: fermentation duration, starter-culture inoculation, ambient-temperature management at altitude. Twenty-two producers presented their 2025 process notes. The published proceedings — informal but specific — have become a reference document for Cauca producers experimenting with controlled-fermentation techniques.

title: Cauca Harvest 2026: early arrivals at the dry mill
body:
Harvest window: October 2025 – February 2026.

Early arrivals at the regional dry mills show flowering happened slightly later than the 2024 average — a pattern consistent with the past three seasons. Cup-quality early reads point toward a higher average than 2025. The municipal cooperatives' price-pooling discussions are scheduled for late February, ahead of the Cauca Federation's reform implementation.

title: Best of Panama 2026 — auction results
body: The 2026 Best of Panama auction settled with the top geisha-natural lot clearing $5,001.50/lb green. Eighty-seven lots competed across geisha-washed, geisha-natural, traditional-washed, and traditional-natural categories. The panel's cupping notes converged on stronger floral expression year-over-year. Hacienda La Esmeralda placed two lots in the top ten; six other farms placed at least one.

title: Boquete Coffee Week — January 2026
body: The annual gathering of producers, importers, and roasters in Boquete town. Cupping rooms operated at six venues; farm tours ran daily across forty estates. The Wednesday-evening industry panel debated direct trade's concentration effect on smallholder access. The Friday auction-eve event sold out for the eighth consecutive year.

title: Lamastus Family Estates Geisha Release — February 2026
body: The Lamastus family released their 2026 Geisha lots in a limited single-day auction streamed live to qualified buyers. Six lots: three carbonic-maceration, two washed, one extended-anaerobic natural. Total volume across all six: 412 lb green. Average clearing price: $1,247/lb. The auction structure — limited buyers, single-day, no pre-cupping — has become a Lamastus signature.

title: Maya Vinic Annual Cupping — January 2026
body: The Maya Vinic cooperative's annual cupping brought together its highland and lowland producer delegations for a three-day program. Seventeen cooperative lots were cupped by an international panel; the top lot — a 1,540 m Bourbon honey from Polhó — scored 87.4. The cooperative announced a 6% price-pool increase for the 2026 season.

title: Sierra Madre Micro-Mill Cup League — closing session 2025
body: Twelve monthly cupping sessions across 2025 culminated in a final tournament in November. Forty-seven micro-mill operators participated across the season; nineteen entered the final round. The 2025 league winner — a Tacaná-slope farm's 1,720 m Pacamara — earned a marketing co-op slot at the New York Coffee Week event in March. Participation for 2026 is already oversubscribed.

title: Tacaná Coffee Conference — September 2025
body: A binational (Mexico–Guatemala) conference focused on the Tacaná volcanic-soil coffee belt's agronomy and economics. Held in Tapachula. Forty-three speakers across two days, including Cenicafé-Guatemala extensionists, INIFAP-Tacaná researchers, and seven cooperative leaders. The conference's published recommendations on integrated rust management are now used by extension offices on both sides of the border.

title: Tarrazú Cupping Week — March 2026
body: The Tarrazú producer association's annual Cupping Week ran ten cupping sessions over five days. 174 lots competed across washed, honey, and natural categories. Twelve lots cleared 88. The closing-day auction brought direct-trade buyers from nine countries; total auction volume cleared $4.3M USD across 41 sold lots.

title: Cup of Excellence Costa Rica 2026
body: Tarrazú lots took seven of the top ten positions — a near-monopoly that hadn't happened since the program's first decade. The winning lot, a 1,870 m Caturra from Beneficio Don Mayo, scored 91.2. The Cup of Excellence panel noted unusually clean processing across the top quartile, and converged on Tarrazú's emerging dominance in the Costa Rica specialty bracket.

title: ICAFE Reform Briefing — June 2026
body: ICAFE's 2026 internal reforms were presented to the Tarrazú producer association at a half-day briefing in San Marcos. Sixty-three producers attended. The Q&A ran ninety minutes longer than scheduled; the targeting criteria for the new support programs drew sustained discussion. Implementation begins in October.
